items.mistake: Using !pip install and then failing to import the package.
items.fix: Use %pip install, which targets the kernel's environment.
items.why: !pip may install into a different Python than the kernel uses.
items.example:
```
!pip install httpx   # installs into the *shell's* python
import httpx          # ImportError — kernel uses a different python
```
Replace with `%pip install httpx` — guaranteed to land in the right env.

items.mistake: Putting magics into a .py module and expecting them to run.
items.fix: Keep magics in notebooks; use plain Python (e.g. time.perf_counter) in scripts.
items.why: Magics are IPython features, not valid standard Python.
items.example:
```python
# in chunker.py — SyntaxError when imported
%timeit chunk_text(doc)

# the script-friendly version
from time import perf_counter; t = perf_counter(); chunk_text(doc); print(perf_counter() - t)
```

items.mistake: Timing a one-off %%time and treating it as definitive.
items.fix: Use %timeit for stable averages of fast operations.
items.why: Single runs are noisy; %timeit averages many iterations.
items.example: `%time x = sum(range(1000))` might show 3 µs or 80 µs depending on cache warmth and GC. `%timeit sum(range(1000))` runs 100,000× and reports `12.4 µs ± 0.2 µs` — trustworthy.

items.mistake: Editing an imported module but seeing no change.
items.why: Python caches imported modules; without autoreload the old version stays loaded.
items.example:
```
%load_ext autoreload
%autoreload 2
from ragapp.chunker import chunk_text
# edits to ragapp/chunker.py now show up on next call, no kernel restart
```
